# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;

class ProductSearchController extends Controller
{
    /**
     * Search products by name, category, and price range.
     *
     * @param  \Illuminate\Http\Request  $request
     * @return \Illuminate\Http\JsonResponse
     */
    public function search(Request $request)
    {
        $validated = $request->validate([
            'name'         => 'nullable|string|max:255',
            'category'     => 'nullable|string|max:255',
            'category_id'  => 'nullable|integer',
            'min_price'    => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
            'max_price'    => 'nullable|numeric|min:0',
            'sort_by'      => 'nullable|in:name,price,created_at,relevance',
            'sort_order'   => 'nullable|in:asc,desc',
            'per_page'     => 'nullable|integer|min:1|max:100',
            'page'         => 'nullable|integer|min:1',
        ]);

        $name       = $validated['name'] ?? null;
        $category   = $validated['category'] ?? null;
        $categoryId = $validated['category_id'] ?? null;
        $minPrice   = $validated['min_price'] ?? null;
        $maxPrice   = $validated['max_price'] ?? null;
        $sortBy     = $validated['sort_by'] ?? 'relevance';
        $sortOrder  = $validated['sort_order'] ?? 'desc';
        $perPage    = $validated['per_page'] ?? 20;

        $bindings = [];
        $conditions = [];
        $selectFields = [
            'p.id',
            'p.name',
            'p.description',
            'p.price',
            'p.sku',
            'p.stock_quantity',
            'p.image_url',
            'p.created_at',
            'c.name AS category_name',
            'c.id AS category_id',
        ];

        // Base condition: only active products
        $conditions[] = 'p.is_active = 1';

        // Name search with relevance scoring using CASE expression
        if (!empty($name)) {
            $searchTerm = '%' . $name . '%';

            // Add relevance score to select for sorting
            $selectFields[] = DB::raw("
                (
                    CASE
                        WHEN p.name = ? THEN 100
                        WHEN p.name LIKE ? THEN 75
                        WHEN p.name LIKE ? THEN 50
                        WHEN p.description LIKE ? THEN 25
                        ELSE 0
                    END
                ) AS relevance_score
            ")->getValue(DB::connection()->getQueryGrammar());

            // We need to bind these manually since we're using DB::raw in select
            $bindings['select'] = [
                $name,                        // exact match
                $name . '%',                  // starts with
                $searchTerm,                  // contains in name
                $searchTerm,                  // contains in description
            ];

            // Filter: name or description must match
            $conditions[] = '(p.name LIKE ? OR p.description LIKE ?)';
            $bindings['where'][] = $searchTerm;
            $bindings['where'][] = $searchTerm;
        } else {
            $selectFields[] = '0 AS relevance_score';
            $bindings['select'] = [];
        }

        // Category filter by name (partial match)
        if (!empty($category)) {
            $conditions[] = 'c.name LIKE ?';
            $bindings['where'][] = '%' . $category . '%';
        }

        // Category filter by ID (exact match)
        if (!empty($categoryId)) {
            $conditions[] = 'c.id = ?';
            $bindings['where'][] = $categoryId;
        }

        // Price range filter
        if (!is_null($minPrice)) {
            $conditions[] = 'p.price >= ?';
            $bindings['where'][] = $minPrice;
        }

        if (!is_null($maxPrice)) {
            $conditions[] = 'p.price <= ?';
            $bindings['where'][] = $maxPrice;
        }

        // Build the WHERE clause
        $whereClause = implode(' AND ', $conditions);

        // Determine ORDER BY
        $orderByClause = match ($sortBy) {
            'name'       => "p.name {$sortOrder}",
            'price'      => "p.price {$sortOrder}",
            'created_at' => "p.created_at {$sortOrder}",
            default      => "relevance_score DESC, p.name ASC",
        };

        $selectString = implode(', ', $selectFields);

        // Build the full query
        $sql = "
            SELECT {$selectString}
            FROM products p
            LEFT JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id
            WHERE {$whereClause}
            ORDER BY {$orderByClause}
        ";

        // Count query for pagination
        $countSql = "
            SELECT COUNT(*) AS total
            FROM products p
            LEFT JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id
            WHERE {$whereClause}
        ";

        // Merge bindings in order: select bindings first (for the relevance CASE), then where bindings
        $allBindings = array_merge(
            $bindings['select'] ?? [],
            $bindings['where'] ?? []
        );

        // Count bindings only need the where bindings (no select relevance bindings)
        $countBindings = $bindings['where'] ?? [];

        // Get total count
        $totalResult = DB::selectOne($countSql, $countBindings);
        $total = $totalResult->total ?? 0;

        // Add LIMIT and OFFSET for pagination
        $page = $validated['page'] ?? 1;
        $offset = ($page - 1) * $perPage;

        $sql .= ' LIMIT ? OFFSET ?';
        $allBindings[] = (int) $perPage;
        $allBindings[] = (int) $offset;

        // Execute the search query
        $products = DB::select($sql, $allBindings);

        // Build pagination metadata
        $lastPage = (int) ceil($total / $perPage);

        // Get aggregated price stats for the current filter set (useful for UI sliders)
        $statsSql = "
            SELECT
                MIN(p.price) AS min_price,
                MAX(p.price) AS max_price,
                AVG(p.price) AS avg_price,
                COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS category_count
            FROM products p
            LEFT JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id
            WHERE {$whereClause}
        ";

        $stats = DB::selectOne($statsSql, $countBindings);

        return response()->json([
            'success' => true,
            'data' => [
                'products' => $products,
                'pagination' => [
                    'current_page' => (int) $page,
                    'per_page'     => (int) $perPage,
                    'total'        => (int) $total,
                    'last_page'    => $lastPage,
                    'has_more'     => $page < $lastPage,
                ],
                'filters_applied' => array_filter([
                    'name'        => $name,
                    'category'    => $category,
                    'category_id' => $categoryId,
                    'min_price'   => $minPrice,
                    'max_price'   => $maxPrice,
                ], fn($v) => !is_null($v)),
                'stats' => [
                    'price_range' => [
                        'min' => (float) ($stats->min_price ?? 0),
                        'max' => (float) ($stats->max_price ?? 0),
                        'avg' => round((float) ($stats->avg_price ?? 0), 2),
                    ],
                    'category_count' => (int) ($stats->category_count ?? 0),
                ],
            ],
        ]);
    }
}
